issue_title: Create a JupyterHub "exchange" service to replace the exchange directory
issue_body:
I would eventually like to replace the nbgrader exchange directory with a more robust solution, namely, a JupyterHub "exchange" service that manages released assignments, submissions, and feedback. This has the drawback that people won't be able to use nbgrader's file management capabilities unless they are using JupyterHub. In practice, I don't think anyone is using nbgrader's file management capabilities without JupyterHub anyway, though (but if I am wrong about this, someone should correct me!). Here's how I will imagine this working.

@lgpage @minrk @ellisonbg @willingc @dsblank I would appreciate any feedback you have on this proposal!

## Permissions

All authentication will be handled by JupyterHub, which will tell the service who the current user is and what group(s) they are a part of. The exchange service will handle any number of courses on the same machine, and for each course, will require that there are two groups specified: one for *instructors* (who are allowed to release, fetch, submit, and collect assignments and return feedback) and one for *students* (who are allowed to fetch and submit assignments and download feedback). This would be configured something like this:

```
c.ExchangeApp.groups = {
    'course1': dict(instructors='instructors_course1', students='students_course1'),
    'course2': dict(instructors='instructors_course2', students='students_course2'),
    ...
}
```

## API

The exchange service will define a REST api that the nbgrader commands (release, fetch, submit, collect, etc.) can access.

### /api/assignments

* `GET /api/assignments/<course_id>` -- list all assignments for a course (students+instructors)

### /api/assignment

* `GET /api/assignment/<course_id>/<assignment_id>` -- download a copy of an assignment (students+instructors)
* `POST /api/assignment/<course_id>/<assignment_id>` -- release an assignment (instructors only)

### /api/submissions

* `GET /api/submissions/<course_id>/<assignment_id>` -- list all submissions for an assignment from all students (instructors only)
* `GET /api/submissions/<course_id>/<assignment_id>/<student_id>` -- list all submissions for an assignment from a particular student (instructors+students, though students are restricted to only viewing their own submissions)

### /api/submission

* `POST /api/submission/<course_id>/<assignment_id>/<student_id>` -- submit a copy of an assignment (students+instructors)
* `GET /api/submission/<course_id>/<assignment_id>/<student_id>` -- download a student's submitted assignment (instructors only)

### /api/feedback

* `POST /api/feedback/<course_id>/<assignment_id>/<student_id>` -- upload feedback on a student's assignment (instructors only)
* `GET /api/feedback/<course_id>/<assignment_id>/<student_id>` -- download feedback on a student's assignment (instructors+students, though students are restricted to only viewing their own feedback)

## Exchange implementation

Under the hood, the exchange service will continue to store files directly on the filesystem, but they will all have the same permissions (read and write only for the user running the exchange service). I think this is a better option that doing it with a database because we don't really need any fancy relational features here and this also makes it easier for instructors to inspect files in the exchange manually. If someone feels strongly that a database should be used then I might be able to be convinced otherwise, though.

Regardless, I do want to implement some form of checksumming, though, because I have noticed at least in the current implementation that sometimes if the system is under heavy load that the submissions are occasionally incomplete or corrupted (e.g. missing timestamp.txt or something).

## Existing nbgrader apps

The existing nbgrader apps will be reworked to make requests to the exchange API rather than copying to and from the exchange directory.

One thing I am not quite sure of is how the command line apps get properly authenticated, because the authentication is normally happening in the browser, not the command line. I see two possible solutions:

* One solution to this is to say that these commands can only be used through the server extension, and then have that extension pass the authentication information to the command line apps. This is probably the easiest but then it means you can't just run the commands from the command line anymore.
* The other solution is to require some how that users re-authenticate from the command line. I am not really sure how to this in a general way that handles all the forms of authentication that JupyterHub uses. Maybe @minrk can weigh in on the feasibility of this, but from what I know about how this works it doesn't seem like a particularly feasible option to me?

issue_title: [feature request] Check for default location with regexp server name
issue_body:
Consider we have the following nginx configuration:
```
server {
    listen 127.0.1.2:80;
    server_name ~^([a-z0-9]+).site.com;
    rewrite (.*) https://$server_name/$1 redirect;
}
```
And if it happens so that this is the first server for that IP:port and there is no default server for that IP:port - this server automatically becomes default server for that IP:port. In that case an attacker can reveal server_name part of nginx config with the following request:
```
$ curl -I http://127.0.1.2/ -H Host: --http1.0
HTTP/1.1 302 Moved Temporarily
Server: nginx/1.13.6
Date: Tue, 17 Apr 2018 11:48:46 GMT
Content-Type: text/html
Content-Length: 161
Connection: close
Location: https://~^([a-z0-9]+).site.com//
```
This must be a rare case, and not too much information is revealed, but anyway if you add check for that it would be great.

issue_title: planemo lint XSD check unaware of legacy parameter attribute default_value
issue_body:
While ``<param value="..." ...>`` is prefered, Galaxy still supports ``<param default_value="..." ...>`` for a ``ColumnListParameter``, as can be seen here:

https://github.com/galaxyproject/galaxy/blob/dev/lib/galaxy/tools/parameters/basic.py#L1005

``` python
        # Legacy style default value specification...
        self.default_value = input_source.get( "default_value", None )
        if self.default_value is None:
            # Newer style... more in line with other parameters.
            self.default_value = input_source.get( "value", None )
        if self.default_value is not None:
            self.default_value = ColumnListParameter._strip_c( self.default_value )
```

The XSD should be aware of this legacy attribute, which currently causes lint failures, e.g. https://travis-ci.org/peterjc/galaxy_blast/jobs/167595623

```
+Linting tool /home/travis/build/peterjc/galaxy_blast/tools/blastxml_to_top_descr/blastxml_to_top_descr.xml
Applying linter tests... CHECK
.. CHECK: 2 test(s) found.
Applying linter output... CHECK
.. INFO: 1 outputs found.
Applying linter inputs... CHECK
.. INFO: Found 7 input parameters.
Applying linter help... CHECK
.. CHECK: Tool contains help section.
.. CHECK: Help contains valid reStructuredText.
Applying linter general... CHECK
.. CHECK: Tool defines a version [0.1.2].
.. CHECK: Tool defines a name [BLAST top hit descriptions].
.. CHECK: Tool defines an id [blastxml_to_top_descr].
Applying linter command... CHECK
.. INFO: Command uses deprecated 'interpreter' attribute.
.. INFO: Tool contains a command with interpreter of type [python].
Applying linter citations... CHECK
.. CHECK: Found 1 likely valid citations.
Applying linter tool_xsd... FAIL
.. ERROR: Invalid tmp6TctTh found. Errors [/tmp/tmp6TctTh:24:0:ERROR:SCHEMASV:SCHEMAV_CVC_COMPLEX_TYPE_3_2_1: Element 'option', attribute 'select': The attribute 'select' is not allowed.
/tmp/tmp6TctTh:32:0:ERROR:SCHEMASV:SCHEMAV_CVC_COMPLEX_TYPE_3_2_1: Element 'param', attribute 'default_value': The attribute 'default_value' is not allowed.
/tmp/tmp6TctTh:33:0:ERROR:SCHEMASV:SCHEMAV_CVC_COMPLEX_TYPE_3_2_1: Element 'param', attribute 'default_value': The attribute 'default_value' is not allowed.
/tmp/tmp6TctTh:34:0:ERROR:SCHEMASV:SCHEMAV_CVC_COMPLEX_TYPE_3_2_1: Element 'param', attribute 'default_value': The attribute 'default_value' is not allowed.]
Applying linter shed_metadata... CHECK
.. INFO: Found all shed metadata fields required for automated repository creation and/or updates.
Failed linting
```

See https://github.com/galaxyproject/galaxy/commit/9128311a607ef0c04d6db17060c7762accbe345c (originally only ``default_value`` was supported) and the option to use the more common ``value`` added in https://github.com/galaxyproject/galaxy/commit/40f19d2a0fd4ccfe52b8b13f1a5de75d623951ac

issue_title: Subordinate charm downgrades charms.reactive breaking primary
issue_body:
A PostgreSQL charm is installed happily, using the default of no venv, and has charms.reactive 0.5 installed into /usr/local.

A canonical-livepatch subordinate is installed, also not using a venv, and the charms.reactive in /usr/local is downgraded to 0.4.7. This breaks the primary charm:

```
unit-canonical-livepatch-5.log:2017-10-23 20:43:39 DEBUG install Processing ./wheelhouse/charms.reactive-0.4.7.tar.gz
unit-canonical-livepatch-5.log:2017-10-23 20:43:39 DEBUG install Requirement already up-to-date: pyaml in /usr/local/lib/python3.5/dist-packages (from charms.reactive==0.4.7)
unit-canonical-livepatch-5.log:2017-10-23 20:43:39 DEBUG install Building wheels for collected packages: pip, six, netaddr, charmhelpers, MarkupSafe, charms.reactive
unit-canonical-livepatch-5.log:2017-10-23 20:43:42 DEBUG install   Running setup.py bdist_wheel for charms.reactive: started
unit-canonical-livepatch-5.log:2017-10-23 20:43:42 DEBUG install   Running setup.py bdist_wheel for charms.reactive: finished with status 'done'
unit-canonical-livepatch-5.log:2017-10-23 20:43:42 DEBUG install Successfully built pip six netaddr charmhelpers MarkupSafe charms.reactive
unit-canonical-livepatch-5.log:2017-10-23 20:43:42 DEBUG install Installing collected packages: pip, six, netaddr, charmhelpers, MarkupSafe, charms.reactive
unit-canonical-livepatch-5.log:2017-10-23 20:43:43 DEBUG install   Found existing installation: charms.reactive 0.5.0
unit-canonical-livepatch-5.log:2017-10-23 20:43:43 DEBUG install     Uninstalling charms.reactive-0.5.0:
unit-canonical-livepatch-5.log:2017-10-23 20:43:43 DEBUG install       Successfully uninstalled charms.reactive-0.5.0
unit-canonical-livepatch-5.log:2017-10-23 20:43:43 DEBUG install Successfully installed MarkupSafe-1.0 charmhelpers-0.18.0 charms.reactive-0.4.7 netaddr-0.7.19 pip-8.1.2 six-1.10.0
```

This issue will affect all deployments with primaries and subordinates mixing charms.reactive 0.5 and pre-0.5. If any of the charms depend on the 0.5 API, then the deployment will break. It may not break immediately, depending on which order the install hooks run, but will certainly break when upgrade-charm's are run later.

issue_title: Tests should not be installed, or find_packages() is incorrectly defined
issue_body:
I think *pylint-django* should not install tests (for sure, not under `.../site-packages/test` directory). Please, apply this patch to omit test installation.

```
--- setup.py.orig
+++ setup.py
@@ -7,10 +7,8 @@ from setuptools import setup, find_packa
 
 VERSION = '0.9.1'
 PACKAGES = find_packages(exclude=[
-    '*.tests',
-    '*.tests.*',
-    'tests.*',
-    'tests',
+    'test.*',
+    'test',
 ])
 
 SHORT_DESCRIPTION = 'A Pylint plugin to help Pylint understand the Django web framework'
```

issue_title: B301 gives a false positive for pandas DataFrames, Series and Panels
issue_body:
`iteritems` is a valid function for pandas DataFrames, Series, and Panels (see docs [here](http://pandas.pydata.org/pandas-docs/version/0.17.0/generated/pandas.DataFrame.iteritems.html#pandas.DataFrame.iteritems)). However `flake8-bugbear` flags uses of this function:

```
$ cat example.py
import pandas as pd

df = pd.DataFrame({"A": [1, 2, 3], "B": [4, 5, 6]})
print(list(df.iteritems()))
$ flake8 example.py
example.py:4:12: B301 Python 3 does not include `.iter*` methods on dictionaries. Remove the `iter` prefix from the method name. For Python 2 compatibility, prefer the Python 3 equivalent unless you expect the size of the container to be large or unbounded. Then use `six.iter*` or `future.utils.iter*`.
```

My environment:

```
$ python --version
Python 3.6.5
$ flake8 --version
3.5.0 (flake8-bugbear: 18.2.0, mccabe: 0.6.1, pycodestyle: 2.3.1, pyflakes: 1.6.0) CPython 3.6.5 on Darwin
```

issue_title: ClassAnalysis.get_xref_to() and from() return empty dictionaries, had to call Analysis.create_xref() manually
issue_body:
Hello,
While trying to xref from ClassAnalysis, I was getting empty dictionaries and also the list of methods was empty.  Below is the code snippet:
```
apk = APK(path_to_apk)
vm = DalvikVMFormat(apk.get_dex())  #
vmx = Analysis(vm)  # Analysis object
#vmx.create_xref() #Without this cx.xref* and cx.methods are empty
classes = vm.get_classes() # ClassDefItem object


for class_ in classes[:1]:
    cx = vmx.get_class_analysis(class_.get_name()) # ClassAnalysis object

    print(class_.get_name())
    print(cx.get_xref_from().values())
    print(cx.get_xref_to().values())
    print(len(cx.get_methods()))
    print(len(class_.get_methods()))

```

I don't know if this is something forgotten by the developers or left due to some reason. I was expecting that all the objects of ClassAnalysis in an Analysis object would be properly populated.

Use setuptools_scm for versioning

So we don't have to manually track versions.

Make sure to use method use by pytest of generating a `_version.py` file and importing it to obtain the version; the alternate method of using `pkg_resources` does not work from within frozen executables.

issue_title: Add ability to create custom assets
issue_body:
If such ability already exist and I missed it, I apologize.
I have some api calls using `requests` module. I want to add a CURL format of the request when the test fails.
I use the `metadata` plugin to inject my string:
```python
req = e.response.request
metadata['curl'] = request_to_curl(req)
```
Then I'm overriding the table as such:
```python
@pytest.mark.hookwrapper
def pytest_runtest_makereport(item, call):
    pytest_html = item.config.pluginmanager.getplugin('html')
    outcome = yield
    report = outcome.get_result()
    extra = getattr(report, 'extra', [])
    if report.when == 'call':
        xfail = hasattr(report, 'wasxfail')
        if (report.skipped and xfail) or (report.failed and not xfail):
            # only add curl on failure
            if item.config._metadata.get('curl'):
                report.curl = pytest_html.extras.text(item.config._metadata.get('curl'))
        report.extra = extra


@pytest.mark.optionalhook
def pytest_html_results_table_header(cells):
    cells.insert(3, html.th('CURL'))
    cells.pop()


@pytest.mark.optionalhook
def pytest_html_results_table_row(report, cells):
    cells.insert(3, html.td(report.curl, class_='col-links'))
    cells.pop()
```
This return the unprepared asset, which I would like to run using [create_asset](https://github.com/pytest-dev/pytest-html/blob/master/pytest_html/plugin.py#L137).
Obviously I can duplicate its code, but if there was a way to do this natively, it would be great.

issue_title: Better formatting for /stats and similiar endpoints
issue_body:
Apart from /stats we have /sys/hostInfo and /sys/memory endpoints which present data in a specific format. While we have a utility that will format that data if the user chooses to do so, it would be good if the output is streamlined and introduced into SDK as perhaps a new method to be called upon, we have to do this in a separate method as not to break backwards compatibility.

Opening this issue for discussion and proposed work

issue_title: Logout doesn't handle bad or dummy Delete request
issue_body:
A couple of inputs on handling the log out: 1. It doesn't throw an error if the session location doesn't exist (Use case where the server is restarted and old session are purged) 2. It allows a user to set any dummy id As an end user, we would want to know for sure if the session is deleted for real or not. Example: ``` import redfish robj = redfish.redfish_client(base_url="https://xx.xx.xx.xx/", username="root", password="0penBmc", default_prefix='/redfish/v1') robj.login(auth="session") robj.set_session_location("dummy") print("Session location: %s" % robj.get_session_location()) robj.logout() ``` Output: ``` $python sample.py Session location: dummy $ ``` It just says worked but on decoding the code how the `logout` handles here is what we should probably handle. Now, I added debug exception code and response checks in `redfish/rest/v1.py` Exception: ``` class BadRequestError(Exception): """Bad Request to server.""" pass ``` logout response delete check: ``` session_loc = self.__session_location.replace(self.__base_url, '') print("DEBUG: session_loc: %s" % session_loc) resp = self.delete(session_loc) # DEBUG: Added Response status check. if resp.status != 200: print(resp.text) raise BadRequestError("HTTP Bad Request" "return code: %d" % resp.status) ``` ``` python sample.py Session location: dummy DEBUG: session_loc: dummy 400 Bad Request

400 Bad Request

nginx
Traceback (most recent call last): File "sample.py", line 14, in robj.logout() File "/usr/local/lib/python2.7/dist-packages/redfish/rest/v1.py", line 893, in logout "return code: %d" % resp.status) redfish.rest.v1.BadRequestError: HTTP Bad Requestreturn code: 400 ```

issue_title: Add a reminder about IP address privacy to the manual mode client
issue_body:
People may decide to run, e.g. the manual authenticator from their laptop rather than from the server. This will work fine. However, the Let's Encrypt privacy policy states that we will retain and publish IP addresses associated with ACME validation requests, including requestor IP. This is unremarkable when running the client on the same machine where a cert will be deployed. When running the client on a personal machine, this behavior will be unexpected to anyone who hasn't read the privacy policy.

We should do a check that the resolved names being authorized point at the current machine. If they don't we should provide a notice to the user that their IP address will be published, along with a link to the privacy policy.

issue_title: offsets_for_times support?
issue_body:
I've written a Kafka consumer using the CPP version of librdkafka, and it works great! However, I am currently trying to recreate this consumer in a more light-weight Python version but I am running into some issues with regards to starting from a specific offset.

In my CPP code I used the following calls to set the partition offsets to a before a specific timestamp:
```
partition->set_offset(ts);
...
consumer->offsetsForTimes(partitions, 5000);
```

The `offsetsForTimes` call uses the `rd_kafka_offsets_for_times` function to retrieve specific offset values for a given timestamp, but I cannot find the corresponding call in the Python version of the Kafka consumer. I could not find a similar function in confluent_kafka.c so I was wondering if this feature is support in the Python client, and if so, how to use it.

issue_title: Incompatibility with other mixins
issue_body:
* drf-sideloading version: `0.1.5`
* Django version: `__all__`
* Python version:  `__all__`
* Operating System: ` __all__`

### Description

`SideloadableRelationsMixin` is calling `self.get_serializer_class()` while initializing in `__init__`

https://github.com/namespace-ee/drf-sideloading/blob/960f66542b3903076db02d387a56cd9de66db71d/drf_sideloading/mixins.py#L13

This causes incompatibility with other mixins which might also override same `get_serializer_class` function.

For example if there is OtherMixin like this one :

```python
class OtherMixin(object):

    def get_serializer_class(self):
        if self.action in [u'user_permissions']:
           # ... some code .. # 
        return super(OtherMixin, self).get_serializer_class()
```

usecase in ViewSet


```python
class SomeViewSet(SideloadableRelationsMixin, OtherMixin):
    ...

```



This will cause `AttributeError` on `self.action` because serialiser has not yet finished initialising 

> AttributeError: 'SomeViewSet' object has no attribute 'action' 


### What I should be done

remove `self.get_serializer_class()` call from `SideloadableRelationsMixin` `__init__`

issue_title: A discussion about using `runipy` or something similar
issue_body:
To continue discussion started in #271 

@ocefpaf wrote:
> I used runipy in the past to test notebook output, but the problem, in this case, are that the changes will be hard to make sense (like diffs of base64 encoded PNGs).

>I think we can do an OK job by strengthen our tests as their are and increase html comparisons. I am sure that the _repr_html_ in that case has a flaw that we can test.

>Or maybe (crazy idea alert) we can add runipy, generate HTMLs, publish them, and inspect them before merging a PR.

We have two levels of validation :
* Ensure that python code works without error
* Ensure that the rendered HTML+JS has the desired output

Ensuring the second level is complicated because we have to test the Javascript and the HTML rendering (and your crazy idea is interesting BTW).

But what I mean is still in the first level. We run the example notebooks, to ensure that everyone can re-run them with the master version of the code. It won't ensure that the output is ok ; but it will avoid to have example notebooks that are 2-3 version late wrt the code.

issue_title: [discovery] create_collection and test_document retrieves wrong default configuration
issue_body:
## Description

The `POST /collections` endpoint allows you to specify some parameters in the body:
  
https://watson-api-explorer.mybluemix.net/apis/discovery-v1#!/Collections/createCollection

```
{
  "name": "",
  "description": "",
  "configuration_id": "",
  "language": "en"
}
```

All elements are `optional` (only one that should be updated to `required` I believe is `name`). If you do not specify a `configuration_id`, the service uses the default configuration. However, in this SDK, if a `configuration_id` is not provided, it retrieves a list of the configurations and looks for one with the name `Default Configuration`:

https://github.com/watson-developer-cloud/python-sdk/blob/master/watson_developer_cloud/discovery_v1.py#L131-L142

This also occurs in the `test_document` method.

## Proposed Solution

Remove the `get_default_configuration` method entirely and all references to it. Let the service decide what the default configuration is (configuration is always `optional`)

issue_title: Setting instance attribute with function during deserialization?
issue_body:
I have a Parent class, with a Child class as a standard one-many relationship ("Parent.children").  The Child class has a field call "specialVal", which needs some extra logic when it is changed.  I have a "setSpecialVar()" method that does this internally.  But when doing deserialization, the value of "specialVar" is just simply set by the deserializer, and my method obviously isn't called.

By the time "load()" comes back with the Parent instance, SQLAlchemy's flush has been called, probably for each child, which wipes out the change history and there seems to be no way to get the "old" value of "specialVal", so I can't even change it back and call "setSpecialVar()" to do the "right" thing (the logic depends on the current and new value of the variable).

There doesn't seem to be any sort of hook to define a "setter" for a specific field as an instance is being made?  It looks like "make_instance()" is a Marshmallow post_load method, and is just doing a plain "setattr()" in a loop with the data passed to it.  Would my best bet be to define a custom "make_instance" for Child, copying most of the code but calling my setSpecialVar() method instead of "setattr" when the key is "specialVar"?

issue_title: rusage.contents could contain multiple equals signs
issue_body:
In helpers.py:
````python
    def adapt_rusage(rusage)
````

It splits the attributes in rusage.contents with:
````python
   k, v = attr.split('=')
````
On my system (PBS Pro 12.2 and pbs-drmma library) there is the possibility of an attribute containing an equals signs so it requires:
````python
    k, v = attr.split('=',1)
````

Otherwise it does not cleanly end the job and causes problems in software such as Galaxy.
Happy to provide patch.

issue_title: best pratice: impact of timezone shifts to data
issue_body:
Fellow pvlib users,
I would like to ask how you handle timezone (TZ) shifts of data supplied by the provider in UTC.
When the `tz_convert` is applied, one ends up with incomplete days at beginning and end of the series.

Let's say I have a timeseries for the whole of 2014 in hourly data.

```
import numpy as np
import pandas as pd

days =365 * 24 * 60

rng = pd.date_range('1/1/2014', periods=days, freq='T')
ts = pd.Series(np.random.randint(low=200, high=1000, size=len(rng)), index=rng)

tz_dataset = 'UTC'
tz_target = 'Atlantic/Bermuda'

ts_utc = ts.tz_localize(tz_dataset)

ts_utc.index.summary()
Out[345]: u'DatetimeIndex: 525600 entries, 2014-01-01 00:00:00+00:00 to 2014-12-31 23:59:00+00:00\nFreq: T'
```

Then I convert it to local
```
ts_tz = ts_utc.tz_convert(tz_target)
ts_tz = ts_tz.asfreq(ts_tz.index.inferred_freq)

```
naturally, the values get moved into the previous day:
```
ts_tz.index.summary()
Out[344]: u'DatetimeIndex: 525600 entries, 2013-12-31 20:00:00-04:00 to 2014-12-31 19:59:00-04:00\nFreq: T'
```
This is then significant when one wants to have complete days, retrieve extact time matching the measured data or aggregate to monthly data.

Any hints or help is appreciated. Thanks in advance.

Note:
Although being more a pandas issue, I think this is more prelevant to climate / solar data in this context.

Better message for missing dependency?
issue_body: If a `DependenciesContainer` is not overridden, or if its override is missing a provider, then attempts to call a missing provider will raise `Error('Dependency is not defined')`. Could we add the name of the missing provider?

issue_title: Set VPC ID in configuration
issue_body:
If there is no VPC marked as default in an AWS account, Senza doesn't find the correct VPC.
A possibility to pass a VPC ID to the Senza config would be a nice improvement.

issue_title: Where did HTML/HAL go?
issue_body: It appears some of the documented codecs have gone from the codebase. Did HAL and HTML move somewhere else? These are currently highlight somewhat prominently in the docs.

issue_title: Pip Install Question
issue_body:
Hi, 
I'm new to python and the new Interop. I've installed one of the wheel library, interop-1.1.3-cp36-cp36m-win_amd64, by 'pip install' command. It looked successful. And the test ran okay without error. I then located the packages installed at ...Python36\Lib\site-packages with subfolders such as:
interop
interop-1.1.3.dist=info
...
But none of these seem to contain an executable that can be used the way the previous 'interop2csv' was used. I'm expecting to see an application or executable that can be used in command line. Is there any problem with my pip install? Any other options shall I try? 

Thanks!

issue_title: Feedback on gluon ssd!
issue_body:
gluon-cv commit id 411f61ce63a318100b14ac597e49eafe8da649b5
mxnet version: both 1.2 and 1.3
cuda-9.0, cudnn7.0
python 2.7
GPU: Tesla P100

Try gluon-ssd-vgg-300 on my own dataset with coco format input

1. **orig_height** and **orig_width** should be float type for python2.x
https://github.com/dmlc/gluon-cv/blob/c114f6c4b7d64e00ab2b5be747d5e46cfd68e04a/gluoncv/utils/metrics/coco_detection.py#L184:51

2. **TypeError: unicode argument expected, got 'str'**
change https://github.com/dmlc/gluon-cv/blob/c114f6c4b7d64e00ab2b5be747d5e46cfd68e04a/gluoncv/utils/metrics/coco_detection.py#L128:33   to `sys.stdout = io.BytesIO()` fix my error

3. **Error: num_slice does not evenly divide data.shape[batch_axis].**
In validation, `last_batch='keep'` is used for validation data loader, it will case error when num of you validation data can't be divided by batchsize.
add even_split=False to  https://github.com/dmlc/gluon-cv/blob/c114f6c4b7d64e00ab2b5be747d5e46cfd68e04a/scripts/detection/ssd/train_ssd.py#L123:23 fix my error.

4. Training speed is very unstable with mxnet 1.2,  sometimes 140-150 samples/s, and sometimes 10-20 samples/s, ungrade mxnet to 1.3 fix the problem, and training speed is always around 140-150 sample/s

5. Results for gluon-ssd is better than mxnet-ssd on my own dataset, 3-4 mAP more with coco-metric.

6. FPS for gluon-ssd-vgg-300 is 
b=1, fps=55
b=8, fps=117 
b=16, fps=148
and speed for mxnet-ssd is 
b=1, fps=65
b=8, fps=124
b=16, fps=156

Finally, thanks to gluon-cv team!

issue_title: "String" sort..
issue_body:
This isn't quite an issue, but rather an info request.

It appears Z3 very recently introduced `String` as a sort. (Judging by the commit messages, I'm guessing @NikolajBjorner is behind this work?) This broke our tools, since we were using that name as the uninterpreted name ourselves, which obviously doesn't work anymore. But that obviously is not Z3's problem!

Rather, I'd like to ask exactly what operations are supported for this sort, what the literals look like, etc. I don't think this is an SMTLib sanctioned sort/logic yet, but I very much like the idea of having native support for Strings inside the SMT-solver and would like to find out more. My brief googling didn't reveal any documents or resources to that effect. I'd love to be an early adapter of this logic and play around with it if you can provide some basic info at least.. Very exciting.

issue_title: z3 crashes in free() on t9.smt2 when compiled with GCC-5.2.1
issue_body:
When z3 is compiled on Linux x86_64 using GCC-5.2.1, and run with
t9.smt2 as input, it crashes:

```
  z3test$ ../z3/build/z3 regressions/smt2/t9.smt2
  (error "line 5 column 15: command is only available in interactive mode, use command (set-option :interactive-mode true)")
  ((and a b)
   (or a (let ((x (or a b))) x))
   (! (or a b) :named foo))
  ((and a b)
   (or a (let ((x (or a b))) x)))
  ((and a b))
  (error "line 22 column 4: invalid pop command, argument is greater than the current stack depth")
  (error "line 23 column 9: invalid pop command, argument is greater than the current stack depth")
  (error "line 24 column 5: invalid push command, value is negative.")
  (error "line 27 column 0: invalid command, '(' expected")
  *** Error in `../z3/build/z3': free(): invalid size: 0x0000000002391ef0 ***
  Aborted (core dumped)
  Exit 134

```
The precise value of the "invalid size" varies with each run due to
Address Space Layout Randomization.  When ASLR is disabled, I get a
consistent:

  *** Error in `../z3/build/z3': free(): invalid size: 0x0000000001d8def0 ***

z3 is version 0f93853a4c (2016-04-12).  Also tried z3-4.4.1.
z3test is version 0af68520ca (2016-04-07).

Compiler and OS:

```
  $ gcc --version
  gcc (Ubuntu 5.2.1-22ubuntu2) 5.2.1 20151010
  Copyright (C) 2015 Free Software Foundation, Inc.
  This is free software; see the source for copying conditions.  There is NO
  warranty; not even for MERCHANTABILITY or FITNESS FOR A PARTICULAR PURPOSE.

  $ uname -a
  Linux coral 4.2.0-30-generic #36-Ubuntu SMP Fri Feb 26 00:58:07 UTC 2016 x86_64 x86_64 x86_64 GNU/Linux

```
Using Valgrind, I tracked down the bug.  It happens because
cmd_context has:

`    vector<std::string>          m_assertion_strings;
`

This instantiates the 'vector' class defined in src/util/vector.h using
std::string.  vector::expand_vector calls memory::reallocate(), which
calls realloc(), thus copying the 'string' objects byte by byte.

However, std::string as implemented with GCC-5.2.1 contains an
embedded array of 16 characters.  For strings 15 characters or
shorter, that embedded array is used to store the contents, and
std::string's pointer to string data points to that embedded array.
(This is similar to how 'buffer' in src/util/buffer.h works.)  When a
short std::string is reallocated, that pointer value is copied, but it
now refers to freed memory.  When the std::string object is destroyed,
it sees that its pointer is not pointing at the embedded array, so
believes it was dynamically allocated, and tries to free it.  Libc
then notices that it is an invalid pointer and aborts.

Depending on circumstances, libc may or may not notice the problem,
but it is reliably reported by Valgrind.

The diff below will add a test to src/test/vector.cpp that shows
the problem using only the vector container:

```
diff --git a/src/test/vector.cpp b/src/test/vector.cpp
index 86ae997..4cb70d4 100644
--- a/src/test/vector.cpp
+++ b/src/test/vector.cpp
@@ -16,6 +16,10 @@ Author:
 Revision History:
 
 --*/
+#include<assert.h>      // assert
+#include<stdio.h>       // sprintf
+#include<string>        // std::string
+#include<vector>        // std::vector
 #include"vector.h"
 
 static void tst1() {
@@ -56,6 +60,64 @@ static void tst1() {
     }
 }
 
+// Allocate some small objects and scribble on them in order
+// to change some unallocated memory.  This can expose use of
+// freed memory.
+static void scribble_nearby()
+{
+    long *ptrs[10];
+    for (int i=0; i < 10; i++) {
+        ptrs[i] = new long(0);
+    }
+    for (int i=0; i < 10; i++) {
+        delete ptrs[i];
+    }
+}
+
+// The test fails when this is true, and passes when false.
+#define USE_Z3_VECTOR 1
+
+// Test vector<std::string>.
+static void test_strings() {
+#if USE_Z3_VECTOR
+    vector<std::string> v;
+#else
+    std::vector<std::string> v;
+#endif
+
+    for (int i=0; i < 3; i++) {
+        printf("test_strings outer loop iteration %d\n", i);
+
+        // Append the stringified form of 'i'.
+        char tmp[20];
+        sprintf(tmp, "%d", i);
+        v.push_back(std::string(tmp));
+        assert(v.size() == (size_t)(i+1));
+
+        // On my machine, I need to scribble during the loop,
+        // but *not* beforehand, in order to cause the test
+        // to fail!
+        scribble_nearby();
+
+        // Check all of the entries.
+        printf("checking entries\n");
+        for (int j=0; j < i; j++) {
+            std::string const &s = v[j];
+            sprintf(tmp, "%d", j);
+            assert(s == tmp);
+        }
+    }
+
+    printf("about to clear the vector\n");
+#if USE_Z3_VECTOR
+    v.finalize();
+#else
+    v.clear();
+#endif
+    printf("test_strings: PASS\n");
+}
+
 void tst_vector() {
+    test_strings();
     tst1();
 }

```
When run without ASLR, the output is:

```
  z3/build$ ./test-z3 vector
  test_strings outer loop iteration 0
  checking entries
  test_strings outer loop iteration 1
  checking entries
  test_strings outer loop iteration 2
  checking entries
  about to clear the vector
  *** Error in `./test-z3': free(): invalid pointer: 0x0000000001b55bf0 ***
  Aborted (core dumped)
  Exit 134

```
One fix would be to change cmd_context::m_assertion_strings to be a
std::vector rather than a Z3 vector.

issue_title: Zerorpc errors/hangs when used in gevent environment AND pyzmq > 13.0.2
issue_body:
Install recent gevent and recent pyzmq (15.x let's say).

```
from gevent import monkey
monkey.patch_all()


import zmq.green as zmq
import zerorpc

class Blah(object):

    def blah(self):
	return "foo bar"


s = zerorpc.Server(Blah())
s.bind("tcp://0.0.0.0:1234")
s.run()
```

Run this file, then `zerorpc --client --connect tcp://localhost:1234 blah` and it will hang "forever", but when you stop server, you will receive the message.

Also when you try to stop the server then you will see errors like that:
```
^CException in thread Thread-1:
Traceback (most recent call last):
  File "/usr/lib/python2.7/threading.py", line 810, in __bootstrap_inner
    self.run()
  File "/usr/local/lib/python2.7/dist-packages/zmq/utils/garbage.py", line 47, in run
    msg = s.recv()
  File "zmq/backend/cython/socket.pyx", line 674, in zmq.backend.cython.socket.Socket.recv (zmq/backend/cython/socket.c:6971)
  File "zmq/backend/cython/socket.pyx", line 708, in zmq.backend.cython.socket.Socket.recv (zmq/backend/cython/socket.c:6763)
  File "zmq/backend/cython/socket.pyx", line 145, in zmq.backend.cython.socket._recv_copy (zmq/backend/cython/socket.c:1931)
  File "zmq/backend/cython/checkrc.pxd", line 12, in zmq.backend.cython.checkrc._check_rc (zmq/backend/cython/socket.c:7222)
    PyErr_CheckSignals()
KeyboardInterrupt

^CException in thread Thread-3:
Traceback (most recent call last):
  File "/usr/lib/python2.7/threading.py", line 810, in __bootstrap_inner
    self.run()
  File "/usr/local/lib/python2.7/dist-packages/zmq/utils/garbage.py", line 47, in run
    msg = s.recv()
  File "zmq/backend/cython/socket.pyx", line 674, in zmq.backend.cython.socket.Socket.recv (zmq/backend/cython/socket.c:6971)
  File "zmq/backend/cython/socket.pyx", line 708, in zmq.backend.cython.socket.Socket.recv (zmq/backend/cython/socket.c:6763)
  File "zmq/backend/cython/socket.pyx", line 145, in zmq.backend.cython.socket._recv_copy (zmq/backend/cython/socket.c:1931)
  File "zmq/backend/cython/checkrc.pxd", line 12, in zmq.backend.cython.checkrc._check_rc (zmq/backend/cython/socket.c:7222)
    PyErr_CheckSignals()
KeyboardInterrupt

^CKeyboardInterrupt
Traceback (most recent call last):
  File "cooler.py", line 13, in <module>
    s.run()
  File "/usr/local/lib/python2.7/dist-packages/zerorpc/core.py", line 172, in run
    self._acceptor_task.get()
  File "/usr/local/lib/python2.7/dist-packages/gevent/greenlet.py", line 451, in get
    result = self.parent.switch()
  File "/usr/local/lib/python2.7/dist-packages/gevent/hub.py", line 602, in switch
    return greenlet.switch(self)
KeyboardInterrupt
```

When you comment out first 2 lines (which is gevent monkey patch) everything will work fine.

The failure is obviously related to some issue in gevent_zmq code (bundled with zerorpc-python).

The "solution" is rather simple. Just add 
```
import zmq.green as zmq
import sys
sys.modules['zmq'] = zmq
```

after monkey patch into test file and zerorpc will work again. So the final working file looks like:
```
from gevent import monkey
monkey.patch_all()

import zmq.green as zmq
import sys
sys.modules['zmq'] = zmq


import zerorpc


class Blah(object):

    def blah(self):
	return "foo bar"

s = zerorpc.Server(Blah())
s.bind("tcp://0.0.0.0:1234")
s.run()
```

issue_title: Python3 AfricasTalkingGateway.py Changes
issue_body:
**AfricastalkingGateway.py Fixes**

- line 227 of AfricasTalkingGateway.py - changed print(response) instead of print response.

- import urllib2 is depracted in python3. The urllib2 and urllib in python2.x were combined and split across various modules in python3. Instead of:
`import` urllib2
import urllib
`

For python3:
`from urllib.request import Request, urlopen
from urllib.parse import urlencode
`

You can try wrapping it in a try-except block to check for both. e.g
`try:
        from urllib.request import urlopen
except:
        from urllib2 import urlopen
`
After doing this, it works. Danke!

issue_title: Consider better way to specify commands

The `@command` decorator currently uses a regex; this is fairly cryptic, somewhat error prone, and could be improved.

#789 added a list of command names, which should be unnecessary.

issue_title: Add --no-ytdl to default playerargs for mpv
issue_body:
For the last few weeks I had the problem that it took a really long time (30s - 2m) to start a video playback from mpsyt. Today I realized that this has something to do with the youtube-dl support in mpv.

I assume that mpsyt passes a URL for the video to mpv which can be played directly but instead is intercepted by the youtube-dl hook in mpv which causes some kind of trouble.

Just adding `--no-ytdl` to the playerargs in the settings fixed this for me, so it should probably be there by default.

issue_title: Creating composites post-load
issue_body: I'd like to create composites _after_ resampling, not at load time. This would save both memory and (CPU) time, especially in the case of large native resolution data resampled to a much smaller domain. There's always the option to use `mpop.image.GeoImage` or `trollimage.Image`, but I think it'd be cleaner to use the already existing built-in mechanics.

issue_title: subjob.resubmit() stalling for at least some jobs on the Dirac backend
issue_body:
subjob.resubmit() is stalling for (at least) some subjobs. This was first reported in private communications from a user.

The tested workflow is to run a job with subjobs to completion.
1) Take a subjob which can run on multiple sites.
2) Run job until it completes
3) Blacklist the site the subjob ran on in subjob.backend
4) try to do a subjob.resubmit()

This hangs on an inifinite loop which after exiting with Ctrl+C seems to be due to a subprocess call hanging either not terminating correctly or waiting on missing some extra input.

This should be fixed before 6.1.12

issue_title: Making a deep copy of a backend resets the `id`
issue_body:
On `develop`:
```python
import time
j = Job()
j.submit()
time.sleep(2) #Wait for job to start
print j.backend.id
f = j.copy()
print j.backend.id
```
will print
```
84286
-1
```
and so for some reason, copying the job has reset the `id` of the job's backend to the default value. I found this behaviour since it happens during a small step of the copy process, when the backend is deep-copied:
```python
import time, copy
j = Job()
j.submit()
time.sleep(2) #Wait for job to start
print j.backend.id
b = copy.deepcopy(j.backend)
print j.backend.id
```
It should be noted that `b.id` and `f.backend.id` are all set to `-1` as well so the information just seems to be being lost entirely.

Looking at the tests now, it seems it's failing on [Savannah8009](http://ganga.web.cern.ch/ganga/release/6.1.14-pre/reports/latest/output/Ganga.test.Bugs.Savannah8009__localxml.out) which was the test I was converting when I came across this.

Hi,

I just tried to submit a job with the following splitter options:
```
In [260]:jobs(4094).splitter

 filesPerJob = 100 ,
 maxFiles = None ,

 )

```
along with a few other messages along the same lines. I'm using 6.1.13 as it's the most recent release that seems somewhat usable with Tasks.

This file has a single replica, which was not available when I submitted the job, but I had hoped that having ```ignoremissing = False``` would mean that I got some kind of fatal error (or, preferably, a few subjobs that wouldn't be able to execute successfully until the site came back online).
Am I misunderstanding what the ignoremissing option is supposed to do?

A proposition would be to add its value as part as the response:

{

}

issue_title: [cliquet] Release the permission code as a separate library
id and last_modified should be stripped before validating the JSON schema
issue_body:
Otherwise it obliges everyone to add `id` and `last_modified` to their JSON schema or use `additionalProperties : true`.

* http://spacetelescope.github.io/understanding-json-schema/reference/object.html#properties
* See #256  
* See #548 

```diff
         try:
-            jsonschema.validate(new, schema)
+            stripped = copy.deepcopy(new)
+            stripped.pop(self.model.id_field, None)
+            stripped.pop(self.model.modified_field, None)
+            jsonschema.validate(stripped, schema)
```

issue_title: Crash on records list endpoint when the collection is empty and readonly is true
issue_body:
When the collection has no record and was never reached, a timestamp is stored.

This does not work if `readonly` is true because the user does not have the permission to insert into the timestamp table.

We should not try to bump the timestamp when `readonly` is true.

See:
* Kinto/kinto#433
* https://github.com/mozilla-services/cliquet/pull/649

issue_title: Tutorial on relay error
issue_body:
I was doing the tutorial and found this error on the page with the custom filters  filters http://docs.graphene-python.org/projects/django/en/latest/filtering/#custom-filtersets


The example has:
```
class AnimalFilter(django_filters.FilterSet):
    # Do case-insensitive lookups on 'name'
    name = django_filters.CharFilter(lookup_type='iexact')

    class Meta:
        model = Animal
        fields = ['name', 'genus', 'is_domesticated']

    @property
    def qs(self):
        # The query context can be found in self.request.
        return super(AnimalFilter, self).qs.filter(owner=self.request.user)
```

The ` django_filters.CharFilter(lookup_type='iexact')` will give an `TypeError:__init__() got an unexpected keyword argument 'lookup_type'` it should be changed to:

`name = django_filters.CharFilter(lookup_expr=['iexact'])`

Second thing is the function qs  is not really clear on how you get `self.request.user` it mentions "set in context.user" that is not really clear what that is.

issue_title: Node fields and ids with clean URLs
Batching selects from same model
Make a Relay ConnectionField optimized to work with Query
issue_body:
Graphene has the `graphene.relay.ConnectionField`, which slices a list/iterable to build the edges inside the Relay connection.
But it doesn't play well with DB queries. [It calls `len()`](https://github.com/graphql-python/graphql-relay-py/blob/master/graphql_relay/connection/arrayconnection.py#L13) with `Query` doesn't have.

graphene-sqlalchemy should have a implementation of the ConnectionField that is aware of the sqlalchemy's Query and use it to slice directly in the DB.

For now, I'm using this workaround:

```py
class RelayConnectionField(graphene.relay.ConnectionField):
    @classmethod
    def resolve_connection(cls, connection_type, args, resolved):
        if isinstance(resolved, Query):
            len_ = resolved.count()
            connection = connection_from_list_slice(
                resolved,
                args,
                connection_type=connection_type,
                edge_type=connection_type.Edge,
                pageinfo_type=PageInfo,
                slice_start=0,
                list_length=len_,
                list_slice_length=len_,
            )
            connection.iterable = resolved
            return connection
        else:
            return super().resolve_connection(connection_type, args, resolved)
```

issue_title: Feature request: plot different scalar summaries together based on regexes
issue_body:
Currently, combining multiple summaries into one sample plot requires the use of different summary writers with summaries coming from the same graph variable, but optionally different inputs like train and test sets.

However, the requirement of coming from the same variable prevents merging plots in cases where this is not possible. For example, the A3C reinforcement learning algorithm uses multiple workers running structurally identical graphs, but each with its own set of trainable variables. This leads to different output variables regardless of the input used, and therefore prevents plotting together the outputs of multiple workers despite they conceptually represent the same.

I'd like to propose a simple way to solve this. As an extension of the current behavior, would it be possible to provide a set of regexes to Tensorboard such that any summaries matching one of them are plotted together? Something like "plot regexes", where each regex defines what gets merged in one plot. This would produce a simple yet very flexible way to manage scalar plots beyond the name uniqueness restrictions that Tensorflow imposes.

Another perhaps slightly more complex but also more powerful option would be to define replacement regexes, which then would merge in a same plot everything that has the same name after applying the replacements. For example, the case of the A3C algorithm above would be solved with something as simple as 's/worker_[0-9]+//' to remove any worker-specific prefixes and merge any worker plots together. In fact, if we consider regexes of the form 's/filter_regex/plot_name/' what we get is a generalization of the proposal above, allowing to combine multiple regexes into one same plot if desired.

Any other suggestions or ideas are most welcome.

issue_title: [mle] Ensure that the radio is left in the receive State while Thread is stopped and the Interface is up.
issue_body: Currently, when Thread is stopped the radio is transitioned to sleep.  This is technically a bug as the radio should transition to the receive State until the Interface is brought down.  Ideally, the code that Thread uses to transition the radio into and out of Sleep should be separate from the code used when Thread is stopped.  Furthermore, the code should avoid toggling the radio state while Thread is stopped if possible.

issue_title: Incomplete entries in IOS get_arp getter returns exception
issue_body:
in case of incomplete entries in IOS "sh arp"

PAR000621#sh arp          
Protocol  Address          Age (min)  Hardware Addr   Type   Interface
[...]
Internet  Y.Y.Y.Y             0   Incomplete      ARPA   
[...]


then ANSIBLE reports

TASK [get facts from device] ***************************************************
fatal: [X.X.X.X]: FAILED! => {"changed": false, "failed": true, "msg": "cannot retrieve device data: Unexpected output from: [u'Internet', u'Y.Y.Y.Y', u'0', u'Incomplete', u'ARPA']"}


works fine when there's no incomplete entry

issue_title: load_merge_candidate & commit_config slow (takes 40 seconds)
issue_body:
### Description of Issue/Question

I do a small change of prefix-list (just a single entry) on a Cisco 7600 device. I do this in a 2 step proces. First load_merge_candidate, and after that commit_config. The complete operation takes 40 seconds. See output of AAA command authorization debug to get an idea of the time the different steps take. Is that normal? The C7600 is not busy at all, I have the feeling that some points in the code "a sleep or wait" timer has been built in.

### Did you follow the steps from https://github.com/napalm-automation/napalm#faq
- [ X] Yes
- [ ] No



### Setup

### napalm-ios version
(Paste verbatim output from `pip freeze | grep napalm-ios` between quotes below)

```
napalm-ios==0.7.0

```

### IOS version

```
Cisco IOS Software, c7600rsp72043_rp Software (c7600rsp72043_rp-ADVIPSERVICESK9-M), Version 12.2(33)SRE9, RELEASE SOFTWARE (fc2)
Technical Support: http://www.cisco.com/techsupport
Copyright (c) 1986-2013 by Cisco Systems, Inc.
Compiled Fri 06-Sep-13 02:32 by prod_rel_team


```

script:
from napalm_base import get_network_driver
driver = get_network_driver('ios')

device.commit_config()

issue_title: Improving and expanding the Photometry class
issue_body:
Currently the [`Photometry`](astrocatalogs/astrocats/catalog/blob/master/photometry.py) class contains only simple primitives, with different types of flux/brightness measures enumerated.  This is something that could be both streamlined and expanded significantly.  This issue is a place to record ideas and brainstorm.

* Streamlining: for example, by using [`Quantity`](astrocatalogs/astrocats/catalog/blob/master/quantity.py)) classes which include errors and units internally
* Expanding:
    * Using a new, more general 'brightness'/'intensity' class which can readily convert between different flux/magnitude units.
    * Allowing conversion between flux and luminosity using the distance
    * convert from bands to bolometric and *visa-versa* using more detailed modeling.

issue_title: Suggestions to re-structure 'data_*'
issue_body:
Currently, there are many data modules:
- data_utils.py: a set of utilities for numpy (to_categorical for labels, featurewise/samplewise transformations), for images (load_images, resize, etc...), for sequences (VocabularyProcessor, redundant seq from file, etc...) and for building datasets (build HDF5 dataset, image_preloader array ...)
- data_flow.py: handles a `DataFlow` object responsible for creating the computation pipeline.
- data_preprocessing: `DataPreprocessing` objects to be used with DataFlow.
- data_augmentation: `DataAugmentation` objects to be used with DataFlow.

All of them are located under tflearn root path. For more clarity, we may organize them more efficiently, such as:
```
# Option 1: just moving them to a 'data' folder:
- tflearn/data
  - utils.py
  - builder.py (move here functions to create datasets)
  - preprocessing.py
  - augmentation.py
  - flow.py
```
```
# Option 2: same, but also splits utils into a new folder
- tflearn/data
  - utils/
     - image.py
     - numpy.py
     - sequence.py
  - builder.py (move here functions to create datasets)
  - preprocessing.py
  - augmentation.py
  - flow.py
```
# Option 3: Only re-organize utils functions (data_flow/aug/prep doesn't change, but 'utils' and 'data_utils' are merged into a new same folder)
- tflearn/utils
     - core.py (from tflearn/utils.py) / or base.py / generic.py
     - image.py
     - numpy.py
     - sequence.py
     - dataset_builder.py (move here functions to create datasets)
```

Any suggestion and other ideas are more than welcome :)

issue_title: PyFFTW returning all zero array after transform...
issue_body:
I have a Python program that depends on a Fast Fourier Transform performed by PyFFTW.

All computers that I've tested my program on are Windows 7 or Windows 8 server. 

The program passes unit tests on all computers except one. 

In the case in which it fails, the problem is the result of the FFT: on this particular computer, PyFFTW returns an array of all ZEROS rather than the correctly populated array that all the other computers return. This is not just a difference in the machine precision, which is expected.

Without getting into the actual hardware specifics of the computer that fails (it is Windows 8 server, Intel processor, very similar to the other computers that pass)... what are the possible hardware / software differences that could result in such behavior? Watching the task manager, the computer appears to be doing the work that I would expect for the FFT... the expected RAM is used, the CPU is taxed as expected, the compute time is expected... but the final result is zeros. I am flummoxed. Thanks!

issue_title: Enhance usability allowing password from stdin when not in a tty
issue_body:
Hello, first of all thank you for this project, its really helpful!

I would like to propose a change to increase integration possibilities of this tool with other security tools ( mainly a password manager ).

Currently due to the implementation of password read you face 2 choices:
1. write the password when asked ( which ends with copy-paste if you have a complex password )
1. use the keyring

The implementation is in [`aws_google_auth/__init__.py#L168:L178`](https://github.com/cevoaustralia/aws-google-auth/blob/a6a9176461c695fc3bf1fa90915dbe6dd6163da6/aws_google_auth/__init__.py#L168:L178): 
```python
        # There is no way (intentional) to pass in the password via the command
        # line nor environment variables. This prevents password leakage.
        if config.keyring:
            keyring_password = keyring.get_password(
                "aws-google-auth", config.username)
            if keyring_password:
                config.password = keyring_password
            else:
                config.password = getpass.getpass("Google Password: ")
        else:
            config.password = getpass.getpass("Google Password: ")
```

Option 2 avoids copy-paste but lacks flexibility: if the password changes often, you still have no other choice than copy-paste.  
Option 1 is tedious, as you have to copy-paste your password at each login. Plus working with multiple accounts makes this more tedious. More over the password is stored in the OS clipboard, which is prone to accidental paste in other places.

A general good strategy for managing passwords is a password manager. That could avoid usability issues in both option 1 and 2 ( password is always up to date and there could be no need to copy-paste ). 

Unfortunately as `aws-google-auth` does not implement any non-tty input method, there is no way to directly pipe the output of the password manager in the `getpass` input. That would be really handy, and would increase security.

The objection that could be raised in letting user do this is that it allows passing the password from the command line. In my option however, there could be a way that respect your concerns for password leakage ( please note that copy-paste does not reduce that risk whatsoever ) while increasing usability.

The implementation I'm looking at is along the line of:
```python
if sys.stdin.isatty():
    password = getpass.getpass("Google Password: ")
else:
    password = sys.stdin.readline()
```

This would allow to keep the current behaviour while allowing direct pipe from another software in this tool:
```
$ password-manager show password | aws-google-auth
```
In my option it also has enough friction to discourage users from writing the password in the terminal ( like `echo "password" | aws-google-auth` ): writing `echo ... |` is way less handy than using the interactive method.

Thank you for considering this feature request.

issue_title: csvlook 1.0 unable to read from stdin, raises error "underlying stream is not seekable"
issue_body:
In csvkit 0.9.1, you could do this:

```sh
$ csvcut -c 1,2,3 examples/iris.csv | csvlook
|---------------+-------------+---------------|
|  sepal_length | sepal_width | petal_length  |
|---------------+-------------+---------------|
|  5.1          | 3.5         | 1.4           |
|  4.9          | 3.0         | 1.4           |
|  4.7          | 3.2         | 1.3           |
|  4.6          | 3.1         | 1.5           |
```

In 1.0, this error is raised: `underlying stream is not seekable`. This seems like it would be expected behavior for `csvsort`, but doesn't seem necessary for `csvlook`:

https://github.com/wireservice/csvkit/blob/master/csvkit/utilities/csvsort.py#L31

(actually, is it necessary for csvsort? Doesn't csvstat essentially require the entire input to be read before it can do its aggregation? I guess that can be saved for another issue/feature request)

issue_title: FindNewPython's forces check against PYTHON_EXECUTABLE during configuration phase
issue_body:
A recent change to pybind11 does [checks against a python interpreter](https://github.com/pybind/pybind11/commit/928fff649fa52ebc7b43860a4914f68e320d2bb2 ). The verification done for FindPythonLibs doesn’t apply in the case of [superbuilt python](https://github.com/InsightSoftwareConsortium/ITKExamples/blob/master/Superbuild/External-Python.cmake) (ExternalProject_Add) since the PYTHON_EXECUTABLE isn't ready until compile time.

In my circumstances, I already have ``${PYTHON_LIBRARY}`` and ``${PYTHON_INCLUDE_DIR}`` set.  CMake's [FindPythonLibs](https://github.com/Kitware/CMake/blob/master/Modules/FindPythonLibs.cmake) allows you to specify your versions:

> If you'd like to specify the installation of Python to use, you should
modify the following cache variables:

> ```
>      PYTHON_LIBRARY             - path to the python library
>      PYTHON_INCLUDE_DIR         - path to where Python.h is found
> ```

Expected behavior: ``FindPythonLibsNew.cmake`` should skip ``execute_process(COMMAND "${PYTHON_EXECUTABLE}" "-c"`` and/or fallback to ``find_package(PythonLibs)`` behavior if ``PYTHON_LIBRARY`` and ``PYTHON_INCLUDE_DIR`` is already set.

issue_title: Apache plugin should use mod_alias Redirect
issue_body:
Apache wiki [recommends using mod_alias Redirect](https://wiki.apache.org/httpd/RedirectSSL) directive instead of mod_rewrite when the objective is to simply redirect to the same path on an SSL enabled host. 

This should also have the added benefit of less potential to mess with existing rewrite directives.

issue_title: Break lockstep between our packages
issue_labels: [{'id': 276667632, 'url': 'https://api.github.com/repos/certbot/certbot/labels/area:%20pkging', 'name': 'area: pkging', 'color': 'c5def5', 'default': False}, {'id': 263357278, 'url': 'https://api.github.com/repos/certbot/certbot/labels/area:%20testing', 'name': 'area: testing', 'color': 'c5def5', 'default': False}, {'id': 281347824, 'url': 'https://api.github.com/repos/certbot/certbot/labels/has%20pr', 'name': 'has pr', 'color': '0052cc', 'default': False}]
issue_body:
As part of getting distros to package more of our plugins, we've agreed to stop releasing all of our packages in lockstep. This means that instead of requiring that all of our packages have the same version number, people are able to mix and match versions. The benefit of this is distros (and us) don't have to repackage everything anytime we change a single package.

To do this, we need to:

1. Modify each `setup.py` to depend on the oldest version of its Certbot dependencies that is needed to work. Some digging needs to be done to figure out what this version is.
2. Set up tests in Travis to ensure the minimum versions required stays accurate. This can be a mix and match between the test-everything branch depending on what makes the most sense.

issue_title: Stop preferring TLS-SNI in the Apache and Nginx plugins
issue_body: We should still offer the challenge for now, but we should change the order they are returned in `get_chall_pref` so HTTP-01 comes before TLS-SNI-01. TLS-SNI-01 is going away.

issue_title: Provide a PEM file that’s equivalent to $(cat privkey.pem fullchain.pem)
issue_body:
This is normally how I keep all of my services up to date:

1. `chmod g+rx archive live`
2. `usermod -aG [Certbot group] [service user]` (`-G` on OpenBSD)
3. `ln -s live/…/… some/other/path.pem` where necessary

Many services ask you to provide the server’s certificate in two files: `fullchain.pem` (`$(cat cert.pem chain.pem)`) and `privkey.pem`. Dare I say most services? OpenLDAP is the only one, off the top of my head, that asks you to provide three files.

I’ve found at least two services ([ZNC](http://wiki.znc.in/Signed_SSL_certificate#General_advice) and [ejabberd](https://docs.ejabberd.im/admin/configuration/#options)) that ask you to provide this information in just one file, and in the same order (`$(cat privkey.pem cert.pem chain.pem)`).

To keep these services up to date, my cron job generates a `live/…/full.pem` by combining the other three files, and I’ve replaced their single PEM files with a symbolic link.

When I have some more free time, I’m open to writing a patch to make Certbot generate such a file, but for now, I’ll open this issue to find out whether or not it’s a desirable feature.

Ubuntu 16.04 Certbot 0.21.1 We invoke a workflow where we call Certbot immediately after having provisioned a server in order to secure the customer's default domain, as per the LetsEncrypt mission statement :) However, what we have found is that Certbot will fail when restarting the webserver, for nginx this looks like:
CLICK ME

``` Certbot failed in newawesome Certbot message: Saving debug log to /var/log/letsencrypt/letsencrypt.log Plugins selected: Authenticator webroot, Installer nginx Obtaining a new certificate Performing the following challenges: http-01 challenge for newawesome.sisko.webdock.io Using the webroot path /var/www/html for all unmatched domains. Waiting for verification... Cleaning up challenges Deployed Certificate to VirtualHost /etc/nginx/sites-enabled/webdock for newawesome.sisko.webdock.io nginx: [error] open() "/run/nginx.pid" failed (2: No such file or directory) Rolling back to previous server configuration... nginx: [error] open() "/run/nginx.pid" failed (2: No such file or directory) Encountered exception during recovery nginx restart failed: b b Traceback (most recent call last): File "/usr/lib/python3/dist-packages/certbot/client.py", line 462, in deploy_certificate self.installer.restart() File "/usr/lib/python3/dist-packages/certbot_nginx/configurator.py", line 680, in restart nginx_restart(self.conf(ctl), self.nginx_conf) File "/usr/lib/python3/dist-packages/certbot_nginx/configurator.py", line 926, in nginx_restart "nginx restart failed:\n%s\n%s" % (out.read(), err.read())) certbot.errors.MisconfigurationError: nginx restart failed: b b During handling of the above exception, another exception occurred: Traceback (most recent call last): File "/usr/lib/python3/dist-packages/certbot/error_handler.py", line 100, in _call_registered self.funcs[-1]() File "/usr/lib/python3/dist-packages/certbot/client.py", line 557, in _rollback_and_restart self.installer.restart() File "/usr/lib/python3/dist-packages/certbot_nginx/configurator.py", line 680, in restart nginx_restart(self.conf(ctl), self.nginx_conf) File "/usr/lib/python3/dist-packages/certbot_nginx/configurator.py", line 926, in nginx_restart "nginx restart failed:\n%s\n%s" % (out.read(), err.read())) certbot.errors.MisconfigurationError: nginx restart failed: b b nginx restart failed: b b IMPORTANT NOTES: - An error occurred and we failed to restore your config and restart your server. Please submit a bug report to https://github.com/letsencrypt/letsencrypt - Congratulations! Your certificate and chain have been saved at: /etc/letsencrypt/live/newawesome.sisko.webdock.io/fullchain.pem Your key file has been saved at: /etc/letsencrypt/live/newawesome.sisko.webdock.io/privkey.pem Your cert will expire on 2018-05-22. To obtain a new or tweaked version of this certificate in the future, simply run certbot again with the "certonly" option. To non-interactively renew *all* of your certificates, run "certbot renew" - Your account credentials have been saved in your Certbot configuration directory at /etc/letsencrypt. You should make a secure backup of this folder now. This configuration directory will also contain certificates and private keys obtained by Certbot so making regular backups of this folder is ideal. ```

Of course per Murphys law I cannot reproduce this atm for Apache, but I have definitely seen this happen for Apache as well. What's interesting is that this flips the webserver completely, and I have to issue a killall command and then bring it back up. My solution is a dumb one: I put in a 10 second sleep command, and that seems to do the trick. However, I am curious: How are you restarting the webserver? You're not invoking systemctl? It seems like as soon as Certbot is invoked you're doing something to interact with the webserver. I can't quite figure out what you are doing here, it's all a bit strange ...

  Version table:
     3.0.4-1+ubuntu16.04.1+certbot+2 500
        500 http://ppa.launchpad.net/certbot/certbot/ubuntu xenial/main amd64 Packages

        500 http://fr.archive.ubuntu.com/ubuntu xenial/main amd64 Packages
        500 http://fr.archive.ubuntu.com/ubuntu xenial/main i386 Packages
        100 /var/lib/dpkg/status
```

## Certbot's behavior differed from what I expected because:

Since long (I've seen this be October, 23th, as per @oerdnj upload, but it seems that the issues existed before that), the Ubuntu PPA is providing, beyond python-certbot-* packages, a lot of python3 packages that **override default packages provided by the 16.04 distribution in main** with **radically different versions** (not just bugfix point releases). e.g. python-openssl was upgraded from 0.15 to 17.3 

I wouldn't expect such a behaviour from certbot ppa; overriding system dependencies with custom versions could impact system stability and isn't generally advised. If updated python deps are required for certbot, either a) a forked package name version is advised, or b) the certbot package could provide a prebuilt virtualenv with separate dependencies.

issue_title: Changing parameter from `--cmd` switch on start
issue_body:
I am trying to figure out if this is a problem with MAVProxy or sim_vehicle.py but I think it is an issue with MAVProxy. 
When MAVProxy gets a string for the `--cmd` switch it runs `module load` just fine but if you do something with the params it will say `Unable to find parameter 'PARAMETERNAME'`. Then if you run the same command once it has connected to the vehicle, it will work. 
I have started a simulated instance with the output `udp:127.0.0.1:14560` and then ran 
```
mavproxy.py --master=udp:127.0.0.1:14560 --cmd="param set SYSID_THISMAV 3"
```
and it says `Unable to find parameter 'SYSID_THISMAV'`. But if I run that command after it connects it is fine. So it seems there is an issue with how MAVProxy handles the `--cmd` switch on start. 
Should it wait to run the commands it gets in the `--cmd` switch until it gets heartbeats?

issue_title: Improve the rebalace: old nodes should only give away partitions, not obtain new ones
issue_body: When the new node is added to cluster and rebalance starts the old nodes sometimes get new partitions. In my understanding the rebalance should happen in a way that partitions are only moved from old nodes to the new one (in other case it can result in that old nodes can run out of disk space)

issue_title: UDF parameters are stored out of order
issue_body: We store the UDF parameters in a dict, which means they get serialized out of order. Refer to https://stackoverflow.com/questions/44249714/google-datalab-weird-udf-expansion/44254299 for more context. We should keep the order these are passed in.

issue_title: Add an option to set the default dialect to Standard SQL
issue_body: #40 added an option to specify whether to use the standard SQL dialect, but this is done on a per-query basis. On notebooks with lots of queries specifying dialect=standard each time is cumbersome and error prone, it'd be nice to have an option to change the default on a per-notebook level.

issue_title: Move the batch unlock policy out of the sate machine

Currently each participant calls batch unlock for one [half of the channel](https://github.com/raiden-network/raiden/blob/be2984577321fd67226307b2ee65c914db55adb9/raiden/transfer/channel.py#L1751). The unlock logic should be moved out of the channel code, and the decision can be expose thourgh the rest api interface.

issue_title: sos config --get
issue_body: Currently `sos config --get` can only get values from specific file (`--global`, `--host`, `--config` etc) but it makes sense to `--get` configure value from all these files because sos loads all of them and merge the result to a big dictionary.

issue_title: `to_host` does not work remotely.
issue_body:
For example

```
task:  on_host='machine', path_map='/home/user:/Users/user', to_host=input
sh:
   touch ${to_host(output)} 
```

Option `to_host=input` works because its how to expand `input` to full path name and convert to dest name. However, `to_host` function is executed on the remote machine and does not know how to expand `output` to full path name (relative to local machine), so the path translation would fail.

The solution I think is to 
1. pass all variables `input`, `output` etc as full path name to remote machine (so that the translation can be done). ...
2. perform `to_host` calculation locally

issue_title: value of sos pack/unpack
issue_body:
I started to question if we should keep the commands `sos pack` and `sos unpack` because the former is basically `sos remove --untracked` and `tar czf` and the latter is basically `tar zxf`, but introducing some new commands gives users an impression that the files can only be unpacked by sos and thus unwilling to use it.

So we should either make `pack/unpack` more useful/acceptable to users, or remove them.

issue_title: Essentials list V0.1
issue_body:
# Essentials for initial release August 2016

Some of these may be mentioned in issues related to this milestone.
Make sure all these boxes are checked. If they are, we're ready for release! 🍻
## Documentation
- [x] Getting Started guide x 
- [x] API docs x
- [x] Style guide x 
## Data
- [x] Binary data storage format (e.g. HDF5) 
- [ ] Explicit use cases + format
  - [ ] Array ID names
- [ ] Support for "complex" measurements (not using a Loop)
  - [ ] Modular use outside of a Loop 
- [ ] Multi-value parameters
  - [ ] Tuple
  - [ ] Array
- [ ] Access from main process to datasets
## Parallelism
- [ ] "Foreground" fully functional with live plot x 
  - [ ] No multiprocessing as default, keep in the code as "experimental" x
- [ ] Logging + debugging to file x 
## Plotting
- [ ] Live plotting x 
## Instruments x
- [ ] Handling of massively asynchronous instruments (for example using concurrent.futures interface). x 
- [ ] Channels
- [ ] Syntax:

``` python
instruments.channel_list[0].volt
voltages = [0,1,2,1,0]
instrument.channel_list.volt(voltages)
```

``` python
instruments.channel_list[0].set_async(0)
instruments.channel_list[1].set_async(1)
instruments.channel_list.do_things()
```
## Syntax
- [x] Loop(Combine(p1,p2)[(x0,y0),(x1,y1),..]).each(..).run(..) x
- [x] qc.Measure(vna.s11, vna.s21).setup(..).teardown(..).run(..) and Loop(..).setup(..).teardown(..).run(..) x

issue_title: option to skip building libttfautohint and use system installed
issue_body:
add an option to setup.py install command (or read an environment variable) to allow skipping the compilation of the embedded libttfautohint shared library, and thus use a a globally installed library, provided by system package managers.
 
This is mostly for downstream linux distros maintainers who would like to repackage `ttfautohint-py`

issue_title: Deprecate /api/v1 from Canvas constructor
issue_body:
Currently we require `/api/v1/` to be appended to the end of an institution's Canvas URL when initializing the `Canvas` object.

We don't check if the URL is malformed in anyway, and as a result, sometimes unexpected behavior can present itself. We recently ran into this issue with one of our own applications where the base URL was set to `instructure.edu//api/v1/`.

We should implement a fix for this in two ways:

* Remove the `/api/version` requirement in the URL as we don't currently support targeting more than one API version
* Force URLs to be formatted properly (i.e. `*.someurl.com` with no trailing slash, `https://` or `http://` allowed) and manually append the `/api/v1/` string ourselves afterward

We should also warn the user that `/api/v1/` has been deprecated and is no longer required if they include it in their base URL.

issue_title: Run Arbitrary Code Every Generation
issue_body:
I couldn't find this in the api or [documentation](http://epistasislab.github.io), so please excuse me if this is trivial. At the end of training a model using tpot, I score the generated model based on mean absolute error and mean squared error. The following demonstrates an example.

```python
x_train, x_test, y_train, y_test = train_test_split(
    x, y, test_size=test_size, random_state=seed
)

tpot = TPOTRegressor(generations=50, population_size=20, verbosity=2)
tpot.fit(x_train, y_train)

# score at the end of training
y_predicted = tpot.predict(scaler_x.transform(x_test))
print('me: ', mean_absolute_error(y_test, y_predicted))
print('mse: ', mean_squared_error(y_test, y_predicted))
```

I would like to be able to run these scores every generation, ideally through a function passed to either `TPOTRegressor` or `tpot.fit`. This might look like the following.

```python
x_train, x_test, y_train, y_test = train_test_split(
    x, y, test_size=test_size, random_state=seed
)

def score_tpot(_tpot):
    y_predicted = _tpot.predict(scaler_x.transform(x_test))
    print('me: ', mean_absolute_error(y_test, y_predicted))
    print('mse: ', mean_squared_error(y_test, y_predicted))

tpot = TPOTRegressor(
    generations=50, population_size=20, verbosity=2, each_generation=score_tpot
)
tpot.fit(x_train, y_train)
```

Is there currently a way to do something like this that I could not find in the documentation?

Thank you for any time you put into reviewing this question.

issue_title: Distributed SGD API for Ray
issue_body:
### Describe the problem

We should provide a distributed SGD API as a part of Ray. This should be usable both for large-scale training (e.g., ImageNet), and also for RLlib (improving upon the existing multi-gpu optimizer). It should scale to 64-128 GPUs and provide performance competitive with Distributed TF and Horovod, but integrate well within the Ray APIs.

There are a couple strategies for distributed SGD that we can provide
- ring allreduce
- sharded parameter server

In addition, we will need to include a custom TensorFlow op to allow TensorFlow to read/write plasma memory directly within a TF run. This will also allow for the pipelined transmission of gradients through plasma.

Current status: we are prototyping this, and hope to have performance numbers and a more detailed design doc ready in a few weeks. This project depends on the x-ray internal refactoring to improve Ray performance.
